expectations.text: A verification report file was written that scores module 5 against all 7 standards with an explicit per-standard verdict.
expectations.evidence: module05-2026-07-06.md '## Verdict at a glance' table lists all 7 standards with verdicts ('4. Anthropic + OpenAI | 🟠 Needs work', '6. Tables/diagrams/animations | 🟡 Adequate', etc.).

expectations.text: Review scope covers the module's quiz.ts, lab.ts, and resources.ts/index.ts — not just the lesson files.
expectations.evidence: Files header names index.ts, all 5 lessons, quiz.ts, lab.ts, resources.ts; findings/fact-check cite 'quiz Q3', 'quiz Q17', 'lab:85', 'lab:86', and a [Polish] finding on 'resources.ts'.

expectations.text: The absence of Anthropic/OpenAI code tabs in module 5 is addressed as a deliberate judgment (legitimately provider-neutral topic vs a standard-4 gap), not silently ignored.
expectations.evidence: Mechanical pass: 'Not a mechanical error (framework code is legitimately provider-neutral via `init_chat_model`), but it is the quantitative shadow of Major #1'. Major #1 argues the real gap is the unnamed provider-native SDKs, not the neutral code itself.

expectations.text: Findings are ranked by severity (Blocker/Major/Minor/Polish) and each cites a specific file:line location.
Findings are weighted by learner impact, not padded with nitpicks.
expectations.evidence: Each finding has a 'Why it matters' tied to interview exposure; A2A finding even flags a caveat that it may be intentional deferral to Module 6. Five findings plus a strengths section, not padded.

expectations.text: The mechanical checks.py script was run over the module.
expectations.evidence: '## Mechanical pass - **checks.py** over `content/modules/module05/`: **0 mechanical findings** across all 9 files' with content profile and provider-tag counts.

expectations.text: No content file under content/modules/ was edited.
expectations.evidence: Harness git check confirms eval-1 target untouched. Chat summary: 'Want me to apply the fixes?' — report-only, offers rather than applies.

expectations.text: A concise, prioritized chat summary was produced in addition to the full report.
expectations.evidence: chat_summary.md exists with a scorecard and top findings numbered by severity, separate from the full report.
